message:
feat(wizard): add dark/light/system theme toggle (bd-331g)

Adds a three-mode theme toggle (dark → light → system) to the wizard
layout. Uses useSyncExternalStore for state management without needing
a React Context provider. Includes flash-prevention inline script in
<head> that reads localStorage before first paint.

- lib/hooks/useTheme.ts: theme hook with OS preference listener
- components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx: Moon/Sun/Monitor icon button
- app/layout.tsx: flash-prevention script, suppressHydrationWarning
- app/wizard/layout.tsx: toggle in sidebar footer + mobile header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- GitHub Issue #64 reports ms --version fails
- meta_skill repo exists but has 0 releases

- Check if ms exists before running --version

 {"id":"bd-19y9","title":"EPIC: Automated ACFS Installer Reliability & Continuous Verification System","description":"## Purpose
Create a comprehensive system to ensure ACFS installer reliability through:
1. Continuous automated testing via a Rust systemd service
2. Cross-repository CI integration with GH Actions
3. Intelligent auto-fix for pre-flight warnings

## Success Criteria
- All installer scripts pass automated verification daily
- Failures trigger automatic remediation via Claude Code
- GH Actions workflows notify ACFS when downstream tools change
- Pre-flight warnings are auto-fixed with undo capability

## Architecture Overview
```
Feature 1: Rust Systemd Service (automated_flywheel_checker)
  - Runs daily via systemd timer
  - Tests all installers in isolated containers
  - JSONL structured logging
  - Auto-remediation via Claude Code on failures

Feature 2: GH Actions Cross-Repo Integration
  - repository_dispatch receiver in ACFS
  - Notification workflows in /dp projects
  - Automatic checksum verification on upstream changes

Feature 3: Pre-Flight Auto-Fix System
  - unattended-upgrades conflict resolution
  - nvm/pyenv environment cleanup
  - Existing ACFS installation handling
  - Change recording with undo commands
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-26T19:38:00.516844455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T19:38:00.516844455Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-19y9.1","title":"FEATURE: Automated Flywheel Setup Checker (Rust Systemd Service)","description":"## Overview
Create a new Rust project at /data/projects/automated_flywheel_setup_checker that:
- Runs as a systemd service with daily timer
- Tests ALL installer scripts from checksums.yaml
- Uses Docker/containerization for isolation
- Logs results in JSONL format
- Triggers Claude Code auto-remediation on failures
- Sends notifications via repository_dispatch

## Technical Requirements

### Rust Project Structure
```
automated_flywheel_setup_checker/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── main.rs              # CLI entry point with clap
│   ├── lib.rs               # Library root
│   ├── config.rs            # Configuration loading
│   ├── runner/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── installer.rs     # Individual installer test runner
│   │   └── container.rs     # Docker/podman container management
│   ├── parser/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── error.rs         # Error classification and parsing
│   ├── remediation/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── claude.rs        # Claude Code integration
│   ├── reporting/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── jsonl.rs         # Structured logging
│   │   └── notify.rs        # GitHub notifications
│   └── logging.rs           # tracing setup
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/
│   └── integration/
├── scripts/
│   └── e2e/
└── systemd/
    ├── automated-flywheel-checker.service
    └── automated-flywheel-checker.timer
```

### Key Dependencies
- tokio (async runtime)
- clap (CLI parsing)
- serde/serde_json (serialization)
- tracing/tracing-subscriber (structured logging)
- reqwest (HTTP for GitHub API)
- bollard or shiplift (Docker API)

### Error Classification
```rust
enum InstallerError {
    ChecksumMismatch { expected: String, actual: String },
    NetworkFailure { url: String, status: u16 },
    ScriptSyntaxError { line: usize, message: String },
    DependencyMissing { package: String },
    PermissionDenied { path: PathBuf },
    Timeout { seconds: u64 },
    Unknown { stderr: String },
}
```

## Deliverables
1. Fully functional Rust project with comprehensive error handling
2. Systemd unit files with configurable timer interval
3. JSONL log format with structured error data
4. Claude Code integration for auto-remediation
5. Unit tests covering all error types
6. Create the foundational Rust project with proper structure, dependencies, and CI setup.

## Steps
1. Create project: `cargo new automated_flywheel_setup_checker`
2. Set up Cargo.toml with dependencies:
   ```toml
   [package]
   name = "automated_flywheel_setup_checker"
   version = "0.1.0"
   edition = "2021"
   authors = ["ACFS Team"]
   description = "Automated testing and verification of ACFS installer scripts"
   license = "MIT"
   repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/automated_flywheel_setup_checker"

   [dependencies]
   # Async runtime
   tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
   futures = "0.3"
   async-trait = "0.1"

   # CLI
   clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "env"] }

   # Serialization
   serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
   serde_json = "1"
   serde_yaml = "0.9"  # CRITICAL: For parsing checksums.yaml
   toml = "0.8"

   # Logging/tracing
   tracing = "0.1"
   tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["json", "env-filter"] }

   # HTTP client
   reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "rustls-tls"] }

   # Docker API
   bollard = "0.16"

   # Error handling
   anyhow = "1"
   thiserror = "1"

   # Utilities
   chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
   uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
   tempfile = "3"
   regex = "1"
   sha2 = "0.10"
   hex = "0.4"
   url = "2"  # URL validation
   walkdir = "2"  # Directory traversal
   which = "6"  # Find executables in PATH
   indicatif = "0.17"  # Progress bars for CLI

   [dev-dependencies]
   mockall = "0.12"
   wiremock = "0.6"
   tokio-test = "0.4"
   assert_cmd = "2"  # CLI testing
   predicates = "3"  # Test assertions
   tempfile = "3"
   ```

3. Create module structure:
   ```
   src/
   ├── main.rs              # CLI entry point
   ├── lib.rs               # Library root (exports public API)
   ├── config/
   │   ├── mod.rs           # Config module
   │   ├── schema.rs        # Config struct definitions
   │   └── loader.rs        # Load from file + env
   ├── checksums/
   │   ├── mod.rs
   │   ├── parser.rs        # Parse checksums.yaml
   │   └── validator.rs     # Validate URLs and hashes
   ├── runner/
   │   ├── mod.rs
   │   ├── installer.rs     # Individual installer test
   │   ├── container.rs     # Docker container management
   │   ├── parallel.rs      # Parallel execution orchestrator
   │   └── retry.rs         # Retry logic with backoff
   ├── parser/
   │   ├── mod.rs
   │   ├── error.rs         # Error type definitions
   │   └── classifier.rs    # Error classification logic
   ├── remediation/
   │   ├── mod.rs
   │   ├── claude.rs        # Claude Code integration
   │   ├── fallback.rs      # Manual remediation suggestions
   │   └── safety.rs        # Command safety checks
   ├── reporting/
   │   ├── mod.rs
   │   ├── jsonl.rs         # Structured logging
   │   ├── notify.rs        # GitHub/Slack notifications
   │   ├── summary.rs       # Run summary generation
   │   └── metrics.rs       # Prometheus metrics export
   └── logging.rs           # tracing setup
   ```

4. Create CLI with clap:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Parser)]
   #[command(name = "automated_flywheel_setup_checker")]
   #[command(about = "Automated ACFS installer verification system")]
   struct Cli {
       #[command(subcommand)]
       command: Commands,

       /// Output format
       #[arg(long, global = true, default_value = "human")]
       format: OutputFormat,

       /// Config file path
       #[arg(long, global = true, env = "ACFS_CONFIG")]
       config: Option<PathBuf>,

       /// Verbosity level (-v, -vv, -vvv)
       #[arg(short, long, action = ArgAction::Count, global = true)]
       verbose: u8,
   }

   #[derive(Subcommand)]
   enum Commands {
       /// Run installer checks
       Check {
           /// Specific installers to check (default: all enabled)
           installers: Vec<String>,

           /// Number of parallel checks
           #[arg(long, default_value = "1")]
           parallel: usize,

           /// Per-installer timeout in seconds
           #[arg(long, default_value = "300")]
           timeout: u64,

           /// Show what would be tested without running
           #[arg(long)]
           dry_run: bool,

           /// Enable auto-remediation on failure
           #[arg(long)]
           remediate: bool,

           /// Stop on first failure
           #[arg(long)]
           fail_fast: bool,
       },

       /// List known installers from checksums.yaml
       List {
           /// Show only enabled installers
           #[arg(long)]
           enabled_only: bool,

           /// Filter by tag
           #[arg(long)]
           tag: Option<String>,
       },

       /// Show last run results
       Status {
           /// Show detailed failure information
           #[arg(long)]
           detailed: bool,
       },

       /// Validate checksums.yaml format
       Validate {
           /// Also check URLs are accessible
           #[arg(long)]
           check_urls: bool,
       },

       /// Classify an error message (for testing)
       ClassifyError {
           /// stderr content
           #[arg(long)]
           stderr: String,

           /// Exit code
           #[arg(long)]
           exit_code: i32,
       },

       /// Show current configuration
       Config {
           /// Subcommand
           #[command(subcommand)]
           cmd: ConfigCmd,
       },
   }
   ```

5. Create default config file template:
   ```toml
   # config/default.toml
   [general]
   acfs_repo = "/data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup"
   log_level = "info"

   [docker]
   image = "ubuntu:22.04"
   memory_limit = "2G"
   cpu_quota = 1.0
   timeout_seconds = 300
   pull_policy = "if-not-present"

   [execution]
   parallel = 1
   retry_transient = 3
   fail_fast = false

   [remediation]
   enabled = false
   auto_commit = false
   create_pr = true
   max_attempts = 3
   ```

6. Set up GitHub Actions CI for the Rust project:
   ```yaml
   # .github/workflows/ci.yml
   name: CI
   on: [push, pull_request]
   jobs:
     build:
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
           with:
             components: clippy, rustfmt
         - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
         - run: cargo fmt --check
         - run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
         - run: cargo build --release
         - run: cargo test
   ```

7. Create .rustfmt.toml for consistent formatting:
   ```toml
   edition = "2021"
   max_width = 100
   use_small_heuristics = "Max"
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo build --release` succeeds
- [ ] `cargo test` runs (including basic CLI tests)
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes
- [ ] CLI help shows all commands and options
- [ ] `list` command parses and displays checksums.yaml
- [ ] `validate` command checks checksums.yaml format
- [ ] Config loads from file and environment variables
- [ ] Progress bars shown for interactive use
- [ ] README.md with build/run/test instructions
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI workflow passing

 {"id":"bd-19y9.1.2","title":"Implement installer test runner module","description":"## Objective
Create the core module that executes installer scripts in isolated Docker containers with robust error handling and progress reporting.

## Module: src/runner/

### installer.rs - Core Types
```rust
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use tokio::sync::mpsc;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct InstallerTest {
    pub name: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub expected_sha256: String,
    pub timeout_seconds: u64,
    pub retry_count: u32,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct TestResult {
    pub installer: String,
    pub success: bool,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
    pub error: Option<ClassifiedError>,
    pub stdout: String,
    pub stderr: String,
    pub exit_code: i32,
    pub attempt: u32,
    pub max_attempts: u32,
    pub container_id: String,
    pub checksum_result: Option<ChecksumResult>,
    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub completed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub retries: Vec<RetryInfo>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RetryInfo {
    pub attempt: u32,
    pub error: String,
    pub wait_ms: u64,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ChecksumResult {
    pub matches: bool,
    pub expected: String,
    pub actual: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub download_ms: u64,
    pub size_bytes: u64,
}

/// Progress events emitted during test execution
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum TestProgress {
    Started { installer: String, attempt: u32 },
    ChecksumVerifying { installer: String },
    ChecksumVerified { installer: String, matches: bool },
    ContainerCreating { installer: String },
    ContainerCreated { installer: String, container_id: String },
    Executing { installer: String, elapsed_ms: u64 },
    OutputLine { installer: String, stream: OutputStream, line: String },
    Completed { installer: String, success: bool, duration_ms: u64 },
    Retrying { installer: String, attempt: u32, reason: String, wait_ms: u64 },
    Failed { installer: String, error: String },
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum OutputStream {
    Stdout,
    Stderr,
}
```

### installer.rs - Test Execution
```rust
impl InstallerTest {
    /// Run the test with retry logic and progress reporting
    pub async fn run(
        &self,
        docker: &DockerClient,
        config: &ContainerConfig,
        progress_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<TestProgress>>,
    ) -> TestResult {
        let started_at = Utc::now();
        let mut retries = Vec::new();

        for attempt in 1..=self.retry_count {
            self.emit_progress(&progress_tx, TestProgress::Started {
                installer: self.name.clone(),
                attempt,
            }).await;

            match self.run_single_attempt(docker, config, &progress_tx).await {
                Ok(result) => {
                    return TestResult {
                        attempt,
                        max_attempts: self.retry_count,
                        retries,
                        started_at,
                        completed_at: Utc::now(),
                        ..result
                    };
                }
                Err(e) if self.should_retry(&e) && attempt < self.retry_count => {
                    let wait_ms = self.calculate_backoff(attempt);
                    retries.push(RetryInfo {
                        attempt,
                        error: e.to_string(),
                        wait_ms,
                    });

                    self.emit_progress(&progress_tx, TestProgress::Retrying {
                        installer: self.name.clone(),
                        attempt: attempt + 1,
                        reason: e.to_string(),
                        wait_ms,
                    }).await;

                    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(wait_ms)).await;
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    return TestResult {
                        installer: self.name.clone(),
                        success: false,
                        duration_ms: (Utc::now() - started_at).num_milliseconds() as u64,
                        error: Some(e.into()),
                        stdout: String::new(),
                        stderr: String::new(),
                        exit_code: -1,
                        attempt,
                        max_attempts: self.retry_count,
                        container_id: String::new(),
                        checksum_result: None,
                        started_at,
                        completed_at: Utc::now(),
                        retries,
                    };
                }
            }
        }
        unreachable!()
    }

    /// Exponential backoff with jitter
    fn calculate_backoff(&self, attempt: u32) -> u64 {
        let base_ms = 1000u64; // 1 second
        let max_ms = 30000u64; // 30 seconds
        let exponential = base_ms * 2u64.pow(attempt - 1);
        let jitter = rand::random::<u64>() % 1000;
        std::cmp::min(exponential + jitter, max_ms)
    }

    /// Determine if error is transient and worth retrying
    fn should_retry(&self, error: &RunError) -> bool {
        matches!(error,
            RunError::NetworkTimeout { .. } |
            RunError::DnsResolution { .. } |
            RunError::ContainerCreation { .. } |
            RunError::DockerConnection { .. } |
            RunError::RateLimited { .. }
        )
    }

    async fn run_single_attempt(
        &self,
        docker: &DockerClient,
        config: &ContainerConfig,
        progress_tx: &Option<mpsc::Sender<TestProgress>>,
    ) -> Result<TestResult, RunError> {
        // 1. Verify checksum first (fail fast)
        self.emit_progress(progress_tx, TestProgress::ChecksumVerifying {
            installer: self.name.clone(),
        }).await;

        let checksum_result = self.verify_checksum().await?;

        self.emit_progress(progress_tx, TestProgress::ChecksumVerified {
            installer: self.name.clone(),
            matches: checksum_result.matches,
        }).await;

        if !checksum_result.matches {
            return Ok(TestResult {
                installer: self.name.clone(),
                success: false,
                error: Some(ClassifiedError {
                    error: InstallerError::ChecksumMismatch {
                        expected: checksum_result.expected.clone(),
                        actual: checksum_result.actual.clone(),
                    },
                    confidence: 1.0,
                    auto_fixable: false,
                    severity: Severity::Error,
                    suggested_fix: Some("Update checksums.yaml with new hash".to_string()),
                }),
                checksum_result: Some(checksum_result),
                ..Default::default()
            });
        }

        // 2. Create container
        self.emit_progress(progress_tx, TestProgress::ContainerCreating {
            installer: self.name.clone(),
        }).await;

        let container = docker.create_container(config).await?;

        self.emit_progress(progress_tx, TestProgress::ContainerCreated {
            installer: self.name.clone(),
            container_id: container.id.clone(),
        }).await;

        // 3. Execute test (with cleanup on drop)
        let _cleanup = ContainerCleanup::new(&container, docker);

        let script_content = self.fetch_script().await?;
        container.copy_file(&script_content, "/tmp/install.sh").await?;

        // Execute with streaming output
        let exec_result = container.exec_with_streaming(
            &["bash", "-x", "/tmp/install.sh"],
            Duration::from_secs(self.timeout_seconds),
            |stream, line| {
                if let Some(tx) = progress_tx {
                    let _ = tx.blocking_send(TestProgress::OutputLine {
                        installer: self.name.clone(),
                        stream,
                        line,
                    });
                }
            },
        ).await?;

        Ok(TestResult {
            installer: self.name.clone(),
            success: exec_result.exit_code == 0,
            duration_ms: exec_result.duration_ms,
            error: if exec_result.exit_code != 0 {
                Some(ErrorParser::classify(
                    exec_result.exit_code,
                    &exec_result.stdout,
                    &exec_result.stderr,
                ))
            } else {
                None
            },
            stdout: exec_result.stdout,
            stderr: exec_result.stderr,
            exit_code: exec_result.exit_code,
            container_id: container.id.clone(),
            checksum_result: Some(checksum_result),
            ..Default::default()
        })
    }
}

use bollard::Docker;
use bollard::container::{Config, CreateContainerOptions, StartContainerOptions};
use bollard::exec::{CreateExecOptions, StartExecResults};

pub struct DockerClient {
    docker: Docker,
    image_pulled: std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ContainerConfig {
    pub image: String,
    pub memory_limit: u64,      // bytes (default: 2GB)
    pub cpu_quota: u64,         // microseconds per period (default: 100000)
    pub cpu_period: u64,        // period in microseconds (default: 100000)
    pub pids_limit: i64,        // max processes (default: 100)
    pub network_mode: NetworkMode,
    pub timeout_seconds: u64,
    pub env_vars: Vec<(String, String)>,
    pub mounts: Vec<Mount>,
}

impl Default for ContainerConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            image: "ubuntu:22.04".to_string(),
            memory_limit: 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 2GB
            cpu_quota: 100000,
            cpu_period: 100000,
            pids_limit: 100,
            network_mode: NetworkMode::Bridge,
            timeout_seconds: 300,
            env_vars: vec![
                ("DEBIAN_FRONTEND".to_string(), "noninteractive".to_string()),
            ],
            mounts: vec![],
        }
    }
}

impl DockerClient {
    /// Connect to Docker daemon
    pub async fn connect() -> Result<Self, DockerError> {
        let docker = Docker::connect_with_local_defaults()
            .map_err(|e| DockerError::Connection(e.to_string()))?;

        // Verify connection
        docker.ping().await
            .map_err(|e| DockerError::Connection(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(Self {
            docker,
            image_pulled: std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool::new(false),
        })
    }

    /// Ensure base image is available
    pub async fn ensure_image(&self, image: &str) -> Result<(), DockerError> {
        use bollard::image::CreateImageOptions;
        use futures::StreamExt;

        // Check if already pulled this session
        if self.image_pulled.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed) {
            return Ok(());
        }

        // Check if image exists locally
        if self.docker.inspect_image(image).await.is_ok() {
            self.image_pulled.store(true, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
            return Ok(());
        }

        // Pull image with progress
        let mut stream = self.docker.create_image(
            Some(CreateImageOptions { from_image: image, ..Default::default() }),
            None,
            None,
        );

        while let Some(result) = stream.next().await {
            result.map_err(|e| DockerError::ImagePull(e.to_string()))?;
        }

        self.image_pulled.store(true, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Create container with resource limits
    pub async fn create_container(&self, config: &ContainerConfig) -> Result<Container, DockerError> {
        self.ensure_image(&config.image).await?;

        let host_config = bollard::models::HostConfig {
            memory: Some(config.memory_limit as i64),
            cpu_quota: Some(config.cpu_quota as i64),
            cpu_period: Some(config.cpu_period as i64),
            pids_limit: Some(config.pids_limit),
            network_mode: Some(config.network_mode.to_string()),
            auto_remove: Some(false), // We handle cleanup
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let container_config = Config {
            image: Some(config.image.clone()),
            host_config: Some(host_config),
            env: Some(config.env_vars.iter()
                .map(|(k, v)| format!("{}={}", k, v))
                .collect()),
            cmd: Some(vec!["sleep".to_string(), "infinity".to_string()]),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let id = self.docker.create_container::<String, _>(None, container_config)
            .await
            .map_err(|e| DockerError::ContainerCreate(e.to_string()))?
            .id;

        self.docker.start_container::<String>(&id, None)
            .await
            .map_err(|e| DockerError::ContainerStart(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(Container {
            id,
            config: config.clone(),
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            docker: self.docker.clone(),
        })
    }
}

/// RAII container cleanup
pub struct ContainerCleanup<'a> {
    container: &'a Container,
    docker: &'a DockerClient,
}

impl<'a> ContainerCleanup<'a> {
    pub fn new(container: &'a Container, docker: &'a DockerClient) -> Self {
        Self { container, docker }
    }
}

impl Drop for ContainerCleanup<'_> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        // Best-effort cleanup
        let id = self.container.id.clone();
        let docker = self.docker.docker.clone();
        tokio::spawn(async move {
            let _ = docker.stop_container(&id, None).await;
            let _ = docker.remove_container(&id, None).await;
        });
    }
}

use tokio::sync::{Semaphore, mpsc};
use futures::stream::{self, StreamExt};

pub struct ParallelRunner {
    docker: DockerClient,
    config: ContainerConfig,
    max_concurrent: usize,
    progress_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<TestProgress>>,
}

impl ParallelRunner {
    pub fn new(
        docker: DockerClient,
        config: ContainerConfig,
        max_concurrent: usize,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            docker,
            config,
            max_concurrent,
            progress_tx: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn with_progress(mut self, tx: mpsc::Sender<TestProgress>) -> Self {
        self.progress_tx = Some(tx);
        self
    }

    /// Run all tests with bounded concurrency
    pub async fn run_all(&self, tests: Vec<InstallerTest>) -> Vec<TestResult> {
        let semaphore = std::sync::Arc::new(Semaphore::new(self.max_concurrent));
        let docker = &self.docker;
        let config = &self.config;
        let progress_tx = &self.progress_tx;

        let futures: Vec<_> = tests.into_iter().map(|test| {
            let permit = semaphore.clone();
            async move {
                let _permit = permit.acquire().await.unwrap();
                test.run(docker, config, progress_tx.clone()).await
            }
        }).collect();

        stream::iter(futures)
            .buffer_unordered(self.max_concurrent)
            .collect()
            .await
    }

    /// Run tests with graceful shutdown on cancellation
    pub async fn run_with_shutdown(
        &self,
        tests: Vec<InstallerTest>,
        mut shutdown_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<()>,
    ) -> Vec<TestResult> {
        let semaphore = std::sync::Arc::new(Semaphore::new(self.max_concurrent));
        let (results_tx, mut results_rx) = mpsc::channel(tests.len());

        for test in tests {
            let permit = semaphore.clone();
            let docker = self.docker.clone();
            let config = self.config.clone();
            let progress_tx = self.progress_tx.clone();
            let results_tx = results_tx.clone();

            tokio::spawn(async move {
                let _permit = permit.acquire().await.unwrap();
                let result = test.run(&docker, &config, progress_tx).await;
                let _ = results_tx.send(result).await;
            });
        }

        drop(results_tx); // Close sender

        let mut results = Vec::new();
        loop {
            tokio::select! {
                Some(result) = results_rx.recv() => {
                    results.push(result);
                }
                _ = shutdown_rx.recv() => {
                    tracing::warn!("Shutdown requested, stopping test execution");
                    break;
                }
                else => break,
            }
        }

        results
    }
}

## Test Flow (Detailed)
```
1. Pre-flight
   a. Connect to Docker daemon
   b. Verify Docker is responsive (ping)
   c. Pull base image if not present (with progress bar)

2. For each installer (potentially in parallel):
   a. Verify checksum FIRST (fail fast)
      - Download script to temp file
      - Compute SHA256
      - Compare with expected
      - If mismatch: fail immediately (no container needed)

   b. Create container
      - Apply resource limits (memory, CPU, PIDs)
      - Set network mode
      - Start with "sleep infinity" to keep alive

   c. Copy script
      - docker cp equivalent: copy install.sh to /tmp/

   d. Execute with streaming
      - bash -x /tmp/install.sh (trace mode)
      - Stream stdout/stderr in real-time
      - Enforce timeout with tokio::time::timeout
      - Capture last 1MB of output (prevent OOM)

   e. Parse results
      - If exit_code != 0: classify error
      - Record full stdout/stderr (truncated if needed)

   f. Cleanup
      - Stop container (graceful, then force after 5s)
      - Remove container
      - RAII ensures cleanup even on panic

3. Aggregate results
   - Collect all TestResults
   - Generate summary statistics
   - Return for reporting

```rust
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicU64, Ordering};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::{Mutex, RwLock};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

/// Circuit breaker states
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
pub enum CircuitState {
    Closed,      // Normal operation
    Open,        // Failing, reject requests
    HalfOpen,    // Testing if service recovered
}

/// Circuit breaker for Claude API calls
pub struct CircuitBreaker {
    state: RwLock<CircuitState>,
    failure_count: AtomicU32,
    success_count: AtomicU32,
    last_failure_time: Mutex<Option<Instant>>,
    
    // Configuration
    failure_threshold: u32,      // Open circuit after N failures
    success_threshold: u32,      // Close circuit after N successes in half-open
    timeout_duration: Duration,  // How long to stay open before half-open
}

impl CircuitBreaker {
    pub fn new(failure_threshold: u32, success_threshold: u32, timeout: Duration) -> Self {
        Self {
            state: RwLock::new(CircuitState::Closed),
            failure_count: AtomicU32::new(0),
            success_count: AtomicU32::new(0),
            last_failure_time: Mutex::new(None),
            failure_threshold,
            success_threshold,
            timeout_duration: timeout,
        }
    }

    /// Check if request should be allowed
    pub async fn should_allow(&self) -> bool {
        let state = *self.state.read().await;
        match state {
            CircuitState::Closed => true,
            CircuitState::Open => {
                // Check if timeout has passed
                let last_failure = self.last_failure_time.lock().await;
                if let Some(time) = *last_failure {
                    if time.elapsed() >= self.timeout_duration {
                        // Transition to half-open
                        drop(last_failure);
                        *self.state.write().await = CircuitState::HalfOpen;
                        self.success_count.store(0, Ordering::SeqCst);
                        return true;
                    }
                }
                false
            }
            CircuitState::HalfOpen => true, // Allow test requests
        }
    }

    /// Record success
    pub async fn record_success(&self) {
        let state = *self.state.read().await;
        match state {
            CircuitState::Closed => {
                self.failure_count.store(0, Ordering::SeqCst);
            }
            CircuitState::HalfOpen => {
                let count = self.success_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
                if count >= self.success_threshold {
                    *self.state.write().await = CircuitState::Closed;
                    self.failure_count.store(0, Ordering::SeqCst);
                    tracing::info!("Circuit breaker closed - Claude API recovered");
                }
            }
            _ => {}
        }
    }

    /// Record failure
    pub async fn record_failure(&self) {
        let state = *self.state.read().await;
        match state {
            CircuitState::Closed => {
                let count = self.failure_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
                if count >= self.failure_threshold {
                    *self.state.write().await = CircuitState::Open;
                    *self.last_failure_time.lock().await = Some(Instant::now());
                    tracing::warn!("Circuit breaker opened - too many Claude API failures");
                }
            }
            CircuitState::HalfOpen => {
                // Single failure in half-open reopens the circuit
                *self.state.write().await = CircuitState::Open;
                *self.last_failure_time.lock().await = Some(Instant::now());
                tracing::warn!("Circuit breaker reopened - Claude API still failing");
            }
            _ => {}
        }
    }

    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> CircuitState {
        *self.state.read().await
    }
}

/// Token bucket rate limiter
pub struct RateLimiter {
    tokens: AtomicU64,
    max_tokens: u64,
    refill_rate: u64,        // tokens per second
    last_refill: Mutex<Instant>,
    cost_per_request: u64,   // tokens consumed per request
}

impl RateLimiter {
    pub fn new(max_tokens: u64, refill_rate: u64, cost_per_request: u64) -> Self {
        Self {
            tokens: AtomicU64::new(max_tokens),
            max_tokens,
            refill_rate,
            last_refill: Mutex::new(Instant::now()),
            cost_per_request,
        }
    }

    /// Try to acquire tokens for a request
    pub async fn try_acquire(&self) -> Result<(), RateLimitError> {
        // Refill tokens based on elapsed time
        {
            let mut last_refill = self.last_refill.lock().await;
            let elapsed = last_refill.elapsed();
            let new_tokens = (elapsed.as_secs_f64() * self.refill_rate as f64) as u64;
            
            if new_tokens > 0 {
                let current = self.tokens.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
                let refilled = std::cmp::min(current + new_tokens, self.max_tokens);
                self.tokens.store(refilled, Ordering::SeqCst);
                *last_refill = Instant::now();
            }
        }

        // Try to consume tokens
        let current = self.tokens.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
        if current >= self.cost_per_request {
            self.tokens.fetch_sub(self.cost_per_request, Ordering::SeqCst);
            Ok(())
        } else {
            // Calculate wait time
            let needed = self.cost_per_request - current;
            let wait_secs = needed as f64 / self.refill_rate as f64;
            Err(RateLimitError::TooManyRequests {
                retry_after_secs: wait_secs,
            })
        }
    }

    /// Wait until tokens are available
    pub async fn acquire(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<(), RateLimitError> {
        let deadline = Instant::now() + timeout;
        
        loop {
            match self.try_acquire().await {
                Ok(()) => return Ok(()),
                Err(RateLimitError::TooManyRequests { retry_after_secs }) => {
                    let wait_duration = Duration::from_secs_f64(retry_after_secs);
                    if Instant::now() + wait_duration > deadline {
                        return Err(RateLimitError::Timeout);
                    }
                    tokio::time::sleep(wait_duration).await;
                }
                Err(e) => return Err(e),
            }
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum RateLimitError {
    #[error("Too many requests, retry after {retry_after_secs:.1}s")]
    TooManyRequests { retry_after_secs: f64 },
    #[error("Rate limit timeout exceeded")]
    Timeout,
}

/// Retry configuration with exponential backoff
pub struct RetryConfig {
    pub max_retries: u32,
    pub initial_delay: Duration,
    pub max_delay: Duration,
    pub multiplier: f64,
    pub jitter: f64,  // 0.0 to 1.0, randomness factor
}

impl Default for RetryConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_retries: 3,
            initial_delay: Duration::from_secs(1),
            max_delay: Duration::from_secs(60),
            multiplier: 2.0,
            jitter: 0.1,
        }
    }
}

impl RetryConfig {
    pub fn get_delay(&self, attempt: u32) -> Duration {
        let base_delay = self.initial_delay.as_secs_f64() 
            * self.multiplier.powi(attempt as i32);
        let capped_delay = base_delay.min(self.max_delay.as_secs_f64());
        
        // Add jitter
        let jitter_range = capped_delay * self.jitter;
        let jitter = rand::random::<f64>() * jitter_range * 2.0 - jitter_range;
        let final_delay = (capped_delay + jitter).max(0.1);
        
        Duration::from_secs_f64(final_delay)
    }
}

/// Main Claude remediation client with resilience
pub struct ClaudeRemediation {
    pub workspace: PathBuf,
    pub max_attempts: u32,
    pub timeout_seconds: u64,
    pub auto_commit: bool,
    pub create_pr: bool,
    pub require_approval: bool,
    pub cost_limit_usd: f32,
    
    // Resilience components
    circuit_breaker: Arc<CircuitBreaker>,
    rate_limiter: Arc<RateLimiter>,
    retry_config: RetryConfig,
    
    // Tracking
    total_cost_usd: AtomicU64,  // Stored as microdollars (millionths)
    request_count: AtomicU32,
}

impl ClaudeRemediation {
    pub fn new(workspace: PathBuf) -> Self {
        Self {
            workspace,
            max_attempts: 3,
            timeout_seconds: 300,
            auto_commit: false,
            create_pr: true,
            require_approval: true,
            cost_limit_usd: 10.0,
            
            // Circuit breaker: open after 5 failures, close after 2 successes, 60s timeout
            circuit_breaker: Arc::new(CircuitBreaker::new(5, 2, Duration::from_secs(60))),
            
            // Rate limiter: 10 requests max, refill 1/sec, 1 token per request
            rate_limiter: Arc::new(RateLimiter::new(10, 1, 1)),
            
            retry_config: RetryConfig::default(),
            
            total_cost_usd: AtomicU64::new(0),
            request_count: AtomicU32::new(0),
        }
    }

    /// Execute Claude CLI with full resilience
    pub async fn execute_with_resilience(
        &self,
        prompt: &str,
    ) -> Result<RemediationResult, RemediationError> {
        // Check circuit breaker
        if !self.circuit_breaker.should_allow().await {
            tracing::warn!("Circuit breaker open, falling back to manual instructions");
            return Ok(self.fallback_manual_instructions(prompt));
        }

        // Acquire rate limit token
        if let Err(e) = self.rate_limiter.acquire(Duration::from_secs(30)).await {
            tracing::warn!("Rate limit exceeded: {}", e);
            return Err(RemediationError::RateLimited(e.to_string()));
        }

        // Check cost limit
        let current_cost = self.get_total_cost_usd();
        if current_cost >= self.cost_limit_usd {
            tracing::warn!("Cost limit exceeded: ${:.2} >= ${:.2}", current_cost, self.cost_limit_usd);
            return Err(RemediationError::CostLimitExceeded {
                current: current_cost,
                limit: self.cost_limit_usd,
            });
        }

        // Execute with retries
        let mut last_error = None;
        for attempt in 0..self.retry_config.max_retries {
            if attempt > 0 {
                let delay = self.retry_config.get_delay(attempt);
                tracing::info!("Retrying Claude request in {:?} (attempt {})", delay, attempt + 1);
                tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
            }

            match self.execute_claude_cli(prompt).await {
                Ok(result) => {
                    self.circuit_breaker.record_success().await;
                    return Ok(result);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    tracing::warn!("Claude request failed (attempt {}): {}", attempt + 1, e);
                    last_error = Some(e);
                    
                    // Only record circuit breaker failure for certain error types
                    if matches!(last_error, 
                        Some(RemediationError::ClaudeUnavailable(_)) |
                        Some(RemediationError::Timeout) |
                        Some(RemediationError::ApiError(_))
                    ) {
                        self.circuit_breaker.record_failure().await;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // All retries exhausted
        tracing::error!("All Claude retries exhausted, falling back to manual");
        Ok(self.fallback_manual_instructions(prompt))
    }

    /// Execute Claude CLI (internal)
    async fn execute_claude_cli(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<RemediationResult, RemediationError> {
        use tokio::process::Command;
        use tokio::time::timeout;

        self.request_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);

        let output = timeout(
            Duration::from_secs(self.timeout_seconds),
            Command::new("claude")
                .arg("--print")
                .arg("--dangerously-skip-permissions")
                .arg("--output-format")
                .arg("json")
                .arg("-p")
                .arg(prompt)
                .current_dir(&self.workspace)
                .output()
        ).await
            .map_err(|_| RemediationError::Timeout)?
            .map_err(|e| RemediationError::ClaudeUnavailable(e.to_string()))?;

        if !output.status.success() {
            let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
            
            // Check for specific error types
            if stderr.contains("rate limit") || stderr.contains("429") {
                return Err(RemediationError::ApiError("Rate limited by Anthropic API".into()));
            }
            if stderr.contains("authentication") || stderr.contains("401") {
                return Err(RemediationError::ClaudeUnavailable("Authentication failed".into()));
            }
            
            return Err(RemediationError::ClaudeError(stderr.to_string()));
        }

        // Parse output and estimate cost
        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
        let estimated_cost = self.estimate_cost(&stdout);
        self.add_cost(estimated_cost);

        // Parse changes from output
        let changes = self.parse_changes(&stdout)?;

        Ok(RemediationResult {
            success: true,
            method: RemediationMethod::ClaudeAuto,
            changes_made: changes,
            commit_sha: None,
            pr_url: None,
            duration_ms: 0, // TODO: track actual duration
            claude_output: stdout.to_string(),
            estimated_cost_usd: estimated_cost,
            verification_passed: false, // Set after verification
        })
    }

    /// Fallback when Claude is unavailable
    fn fallback_manual_instructions(&self, prompt: &str) -> RemediationResult {
        let instructions = FallbackRemediation::generate_from_prompt(prompt);
        
        RemediationResult {
            success: false,
            method: RemediationMethod::ManualRequired,
            changes_made: vec![],
            commit_sha: None,
            pr_url: None,
            duration_ms: 0,
            claude_output: format!("Claude unavailable. Manual instructions:\n{}", instructions),
            estimated_cost_usd: 0.0,
            verification_passed: false,
        }
    }

    fn estimate_cost(&self, output: &str) -> f32 {
        // Rough estimation based on token count
        // Actual cost tracking would parse Claude's response metadata
        let char_count = output.len();
        let estimated_tokens = char_count / 4;  // ~4 chars per token
        let cost_per_1k_tokens = 0.015;  // Claude Opus 4.5 output pricing
        (estimated_tokens as f32 / 1000.0) * cost_per_1k_tokens
    }

    fn add_cost(&self, cost: f32) {
        let microdollars = (cost * 1_000_000.0) as u64;
        self.total_cost_usd.fetch_add(microdollars, Ordering::SeqCst);
    }

    pub fn get_total_cost_usd(&self) -> f32 {
        self.total_cost_usd.load(Ordering::SeqCst) as f32 / 1_000_000.0
    }

    fn parse_changes(&self, _output: &str) -> Result<Vec<FileChange>, RemediationError> {
        // Parse Claude output for file changes
        // Implementation depends on Claude output format
        Ok(vec![])
    }

    /// Get health status of the remediation system
    pub async fn health_check(&self) -> RemediationHealth {
        RemediationHealth {
            circuit_state: self.circuit_breaker.get_state().await,
            total_requests: self.request_count.load(Ordering::SeqCst),
            total_cost_usd: self.get_total_cost_usd(),
            cost_limit_usd: self.cost_limit_usd,
            cost_remaining_usd: self.cost_limit_usd - self.get_total_cost_usd(),
            claude_available: FallbackRemediation::is_claude_available().await,
        }
    }
}

pub struct RemediationHealth {
    pub circuit_state: CircuitState,
    pub total_requests: u32,

pub struct FallbackRemediation;

impl FallbackRemediation {
    /// Generate manual fix instructions when Claude is unavailable
    pub fn generate_instructions(error: &InstallerError) -> ManualInstructions {
        match error.error_type {
            ErrorType::ChecksumMismatch { ref expected, ref actual, ref tool } => {
                ManualInstructions {
                    title: format!("Update checksum for {}", tool),
                    steps: vec![
                        format!("1. Open checksums.yaml"),
                        format!("2. Find the entry for '{}'", tool),
                        format!("3. Update sha256 from '{}' to '{}'", 
                            &expected[..16], &actual[..16]),
                        "4. Save and commit".to_string(),
                    ],
                    commands: vec![

                    ],
                    files_to_edit: vec![PathBuf::from("checksums.yaml")],
                    estimated_time_minutes: 5,
                    documentation_links: vec![],
                }
            }
            ErrorType::AptLockContention { .. } => {
                ManualInstructions {
                    title: "Resolve apt lock contention".to_string(),
                    steps: vec![
                        "1. Wait for the blocking process to finish, OR".to_string(),
                        "2. Kill stuck apt processes if safe".to_string(),
                        "3. Run dpkg --configure -a".to_string(),
                    ],
                    commands: vec![
                        "sudo pkill -9 apt apt-get dpkg || true".to_string(),
                        "sudo rm -f /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend /var/lib/apt/lists/lock".to_string(),
                        "sudo dpkg --configure -a".to_string(),
                    ],
                    files_to_edit: vec![],
                    estimated_time_minutes: 10,
                    documentation_links: vec![
                        "https://wiki.debian.org/Teams/Dpkg".to_string(),
                    ],
                }
            }
            _ => ManualInstructions {
                title: "Manual investigation required".to_string(),
                steps: vec![
                    "1. Check the error logs".to_string(),
                    "2. Identify the root cause".to_string(),
                    "3. Apply appropriate fix".to_string(),
                ],
                commands: vec![],
                files_to_edit: vec![],
                estimated_time_minutes: 30,
                documentation_links: vec![],
            },
        }
    }

    /// Generate instructions from raw prompt (when error details unavailable)
    pub fn generate_from_prompt(prompt: &str) -> String {
        format!(
            "Claude is currently unavailable. Please review the following manually:\n\n{}\n\nOnce you've made changes, re-run the verification.", 
            prompt
        )
    }

    /// Check if Claude CLI is available and authenticated
    pub async fn is_claude_available() -> bool {
        use tokio::process::Command;
        
        let output = Command::new("claude")
            .arg("--version")
            .output()
            .await;
        
        match output {
            Ok(o) => o.status.success(),
            Err(_) => false,
        }
    }

    /// Get reason why Claude is unavailable
    pub async fn get_unavailability_reason() -> Option<String> {
        use tokio::process::Command;
        
        let output = Command::new("claude")
            .arg("--version")
            .output()
            .await;
        
        match output {
            Ok(o) if !o.status.success() => {
                Some(String::from_utf8_lossy(&o.stderr).to_string())
            }
            Err(e) => Some(format!("Claude CLI not found: {}", e)),
            Ok(_) => None,
        }
    }
}
